ev.headline: Ford EV Chief Doug Field Leaves Automaker Amid Major Restructuring
ev.summary: Ford's head of electric vehicles Doug Field is departing the company after a transition period, with Ford restructuring its EV strategy and planning major product launches including the Universal Electric Vehicle platform.

ev.headline: Greater Bay Technology Achieves All-Solid-State Battery Production Milestone
ev.summary: Chinese battery maker Greater Bay Technology announced its first A-sample all-solid-state battery cells passed safety tests, aiming to launch mass production in 2026.

ev.headline: CATL Partners with SAIC-GM-Wuling for Fast-Charging Technology Development
ev.summary: Battery leader CATL announced a partnership to develop ultra-fast charging technology and battery-swap-compatible vehicles, challenging BYD's charging innovations.

ev.headline: Mercedes-Benz Unveils Heavily Upgraded EQS with 575-Mile Range
ev.summary: Mercedes unveiled an overhauled EQS electric sedan with 926 km range, 800-volt architecture, and up to 350 kW DC charging, representing the biggest upgrade since its 2021 launch.

ev.headline: Rivian Partners with Redwood Materials for Battery Recycling and Energy Storage
ev.summary: Rivian signed a deal with battery-recycling firm Redwood Materials to convert used EV batteries into stationary energy storage, starting with 10 megawatt-hours at its Illinois facility.

ev.headline: Tesla Full Self-Driving Wins First European Regulatory Approval in Netherlands
ev.summary: The Dutch vehicle authority RDW approved Tesla's Full Self-Driving Supervised system, marking the first European country to officially approve the driver-assist technology.

ev.headline: Lucid Motors Delivers 3,093 Vehicles in Q1 2026 Amid Gravity SUV Stop-Sale
ev.summary: Saudi-backed Lucid Motors disclosed Q1 deliveries of 3,093 units globally, impacted by a 29-day stop-sale on the Gravity SUV during the quarter.

ev.headline: Volkswagen Ends ID.4 Production in US, Shifts to Atlas Models
ev.summary: Volkswagen is halting ID.4 assembly at its Chattanooga plant in mid-April 2026, redirecting production toward the all-new 2027 Atlas and exploring new U.S. market models.

ev.headline: EV Sales Fall 27% Year-Over-Year in Q1 2026, But Stabilize Near 6%
ev.summary: Electric vehicle sales declined 27% YoY in Q1 2026 to 216,399 units, but the pace of decline slowed from Q4 2025, suggesting stabilization after government incentive expiration.

ev.headline: Used EV Sales Surge 12% in Q1 2026 as Off-Lease Vehicles Hit Market
ev.summary: Used electric vehicle sales increased 12% in the first quarter of 2026, driven by a wave of off-lease EVs entering the market at attractive prices.

ev.headline: US EV Adoption Faces Headwinds from Policy Shifts and Expiring Tax Credits
ev.summary: U.S. EV momentum slowed after the $7,500 federal tax credit expired in September 2025 and charging infrastructure funding was frozen, with new tariffs adding uncertainty.

ev.headline: Kia EV3 Coming to US Market Late 2026 with Affordable Pricing
ev.summary: Kia announced it will bring the EV3 electric subcompact crossover to the U.S. later in 2026, targeting entry-level buyers and competing on affordability.

ev.headline: Denza Z9GT Launches in Europe with 10-Minute Ultra-Fast Charging
ev.summary: BYD's Denza brand launched the Z9GT shooting brake in Europe with Flash Charging technology enabling 10%-97% charge in under 10 minutes.

ev.headline: Global EV Sales Forecast Revised Downward to 27.5% Market Share by 2026
ev.summary: EV Volumes forecasts global EV sales at 27.5% market share in 2026, down from earlier expectations, reflecting policy shifts and trade uncertainty.

ev.headline: Automakers Explore Stripped-Down EVs to Compete with Chinese Manufacturers
ev.summary: Dodge and Slate are exploring 'less-for-less' EV designs removing features like radios and digital gauges to hit lower price points and compete with affordable Chinese EVs.

ev.headline: Kia Hits 100,000 EV Sales Milestone in UK, First Korean Brand Achievement
ev.summary: Kia became the first Korean automaker to sell 100,000 electric vehicles in the UK, as the Niro EV exited the market.

ev.headline: Federal EV Charger Tax Credit Set to Expire June 30, 2026
ev.summary: The federal tax credit for home EV chargers, covering up to 30% of installation costs (capped at $1,000), is scheduled to expire on June 30, 2026.

ev.headline: March EV Sales Show Signs of Stabilization with 20% Month-Over-Month Growth
ev.summary: EV sales in March totaled 82,629 units, down 24.7% year-over-year but up 20.2% month-over-month, accounting for 5.9% of total vehicle sales.

ev.headline: Stellantis Takes $26.5 Billion Charge on EV Production Cuts
ev.summary: Stellantis announced a massive $26.5 billion charge as it cuts electric vehicle production after misjudging consumer demand, joining Ford and GM in major EV write-downs.

ev.headline: Volvo Launches Production of Electric Articulated Haul Trucks
ev.summary: Volvo began regular production of its new A30 and A40 Electric articulated haul trucks, marking the first time electric haulers of this size moved into serial production.

ev.headline: Toyota bZ Becomes Third-Best-Selling EV in US for Q1 2026
ev.summary: Toyota's bZ electric SUV surpassed the Hyundai IONIQ 5, Chevy Equinox EV, and Ford Mustang Mach-E to rank as the third-best-selling EV in the U.S. during Q1.

ev.headline: DC Fast Chargers Expand 30% Nationwide Despite EV Sales Slowdown
ev.summary: More than 18,000 direct current fast charging ports were installed nationwide in 2025, a 30% increase over 2024, even as EV adoption slowed.

ev.headline: CATL Leads Global EV Battery Market with 39.2% Share in 2025
ev.summary: CATL maintained its dominance in global EV battery sales with 39.2% market share in 2025, followed by BYD at 16.4% and LG Energy Solution at 9.2%.

ev.headline: Tesla Model Y Dominates US EV Market, Capturing 33% of Q1 Sales
ev.summary: One out of every three electric vehicles sold in Q1 2026 was a Tesla Model Y, with the sedan seeing strong sales growth despite overall market weakness.

ev.headline: Rove Opens Second EV Charging Center in California with 40 DC Fast Chargers
ev.summary: Rove opened its second full-service EV charging facility in Costa Mesa, California, featuring 40 DC fast chargers and amenities, with five additional locations planned.

energy.headline: Why More Texans Are Taking Their Power Back
energy.summary: Texas families increasingly pair solar with storage as energy costs rise, grid pressure grows, and extreme weather becomes more frequent.

energy.headline: Solar Incentives: How to save money on solar panels in 2026
energy.summary: Federal residential tax credit for home batteries expired December 31, 2025; state and utility incentives now lead savings opportunities.

energy.headline: Batteries buying 'free' California solar, driving up price
energy.summary: Energy storage systems are raising the value of negatively priced solar electricity by up to $42 per MWh in California's wholesale market.

energy.headline: US solar module prices face upward pressure
energy.summary: Median solar module pricing reached $0.28 per watt in Q1 2026 due to trade enforcement and Foreign Entity of Concern compliance requirements.

energy.headline: Massachusetts initiative helping nonprofits access federal tax credits for solar
energy.summary: Resonant Energy's Solar Upgrading Nonprofits program helps resource-strapped nonprofits access federal tax credits before they disappear.

energy.headline: EIA: US to add record 43.4GW of new utility-scale solar PV capacity in 2026
energy.summary: US expects to add record 43.4GW of utility-scale solar capacity in 2026, a 60% increase over 2025.

energy.headline: U.S. solar and storage market report: 2026 state rankings and forecasts
energy.summary: Comprehensive breakdown of US solar and battery storage landscape showing state-level performance metrics and future pipelines.

energy.headline: Bluetti launches new EnergyPro 13K energy storage system in the U.S.
energy.summary: Bluetti introduces modular EnergyPro 13K home battery system starting at $7,919 with bonuses through April 24.

energy.headline: Expiring incentives led to record demand in residential solar market
energy.summary: Federal tax credit expiration triggered 205% surge in homeowner engagement with solar installers in H2 2025.

energy.headline: Solar installs surged 205% before the tax credit cut
energy.summary: US homeowners rushed to install solar before federal tax credits ended, driving historic 205% demand surge in late 2025.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves
energy.summary: Net metering policy changes and rising electricity prices are shifting homeowners toward battery storage over grid-tied solar.

energy.headline: Net metering for solar owners suffers defeat in California courts
energy.summary: California appeals court upheld 2022 decision reducing rooftop solar payments by 75%, limiting grid export economics.

energy.headline: How Colorado Net Metering Works for Solar Homeowners in 2026
energy.summary: Colorado maintains strong net metering policies with full retail rates and additional battery incentives through Xcel Energy programs.

energy.headline: Energy Storage Market Outlook Report shows 50% residential growth
energy.summary: Residential battery storage grew 50% due to expiration of 25D residential solar tax credit, with EV market shifts accelerating projections.

energy.headline: Tigo Energy Expands Residential Solar Repowering with New Inverter Controls
energy.summary: Tigo Energy launches Inverter Power Output Control for 3.8kW EI inverter to support legacy system repowering without re-permitting.

energy.headline: US Residential Solar Enters Post-Incentive Era After ITC Expiry Surge
energy.summary: Third-party ownership options like leases and PPAs gaining market share as residential solar transitions to post-incentive phase.

energy.headline: Maine becomes sixth state to legalize plug-in solar panels
energy.summary: Governor Janet Mills signed LD 1730 allowing installation of plug-in solar systems up to 1,200 watts.

energy.headline: Residential Solar Energy Storage Market Valued at $72.8 Billion in 2025
energy.summary: Market projected to reach $462.34 billion by 2036 at 18.3% CAGR, driven by declining grid export economics.

energy.headline: Global solar energy storage battery market to grow from $7.84B to $59.82B by 2034
energy.summary: Market projected to exhibit 28.93% CAGR, with Asia Pacific dominating at 54.40% market share.

energy.headline: EnergySage Marketplace Report Shows Incentive Expiration Triggered Historic Shift
energy.summary: Federal tax credit deadline created record surge in solar demand and deferred battery adoption, creating retrofit opportunity.

energy.headline: Why 2026 Could Be A Bright Year For U.S. Solar
energy.summary: US solar capacity expected to rebound despite trade and permitting challenges, with data center demand driving growth.

v2x.headline: Rivian and Redwood Materials Partner for Second-Life EV Battery Grid Storage
v2x.summary: Rivian partners with Redwood Materials on a 10-MWh battery system using repurposed EV batteries to cut costs and boost grid stability at Illinois plant.

v2x.headline: Rivian Taps Tesla Co-Founder's Redwood Materials To Turn Used EV Batteries Into Grid Power
v2x.summary: Rivian and Redwood partner on 10 MWh battery system using repurposed EV packs to cut costs and boost grid stability at Illinois plant.

v2x.headline: California Initiative May Accelerate North America's Adoption of Bidirectional EV Charging
v2x.summary: California Energy Commission released roadmap showing EVs could provide 5 GW peak grid reduction with potential $262-321 annual savings per vehicle by 2030.

v2x.headline: Yes, an EV really CAN power your home – if it's one of these [update]
v2x.summary: Latest update on V2H-capable EVs including Volvo EX90, Polestar 3, Tesla Cybertruck, and others rolling out bidirectional charging in 2026.

v2x.headline: GM leads the charge in vehicle-to-home EVs: inside the home energy ecosystem
v2x.summary: GM sold 246,000 V2H-capable EVs in 2025 and explores leasing program for home energy management systems to make bidirectional charging more accessible.

v2x.headline: Nissan to launch affordable vehicle-to-grid technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announced affordable bi-directional charging on selected EVs in 2026, building on its extensive V2G pilot programs and G99 UK grid certification.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils New IQ Bidirectional EV Charger Architecture
v2x.summary: Enphase unveiled IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G with up to 11.5kW power, AI-managed optimization, expected Q2 2026 availability.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Home is the Bidirectional Upgrade You'll Use in 2026
v2x.summary: V2H offers simpler alternative to V2G without grid interconnection complexity, becoming viable backup power solution for Australian and global homeowners.

v2x.headline: As EV load grows, utilities use managed charging to harness flexibility, lower costs
v2x.summary: PG&E scales V1G programs to 50,000 customers, with industry shift from V1G to V2G creating standardized protocols for grid integration at scale.

v2x.headline: Retired EV Batteries Scored a New Gig: Bolstering Texas' Grid
v2x.summary: Retired EV batteries from GM vehicles now power Texas grid through B2U Storage, enabling cost-effective energy storage and grid stabilization in ERCOT region.

v2x.headline: Landmark V2G Technology Demonstration during Climate Week NYC
v2x.summary: Fermata Energy demonstrated V2G peak load reduction of 14.6kW with $518 monthly savings potential using Nissan LEAF and 5G-enabled solar charging.

v2x.headline: Nio Participates in Vehicle-to-Grid Demonstration Discharge with Over 100 EVs
v2x.summary: Nio mobilized 104 vehicles in Tianjin for V2G discharge demonstration, achieving 1.741 MW peak output across four charging stations simultaneously.

v2x.headline: Top 10 EV Battery Trends in 2025 and What We Can Expect in 2026
v2x.summary: Enhanced grid integration with machine-learning charging systems enable EVs to send energy back during peak demand, reducing infrastructure upgrade costs.

v2x.headline: How electric vehicles could back up our power system
v2x.summary: California study shows proactive grid upgrades could reduce V2G costs to $0.12-0.18/day, enable 59,200 metric tons annual CO2 reductions by 2045.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging (V2G) in 2026: Which Cars Support It?
v2x.summary: 2026 breakthrough year for bidirectional charging with Ford F-150 Lightning, Tesla Cybertruck, GM Ultium, Mitsubishi Outlander, and Volvo EX90 leading adoption.

v2x.headline: The Driveway Dividend and How Bidirectional V2G Technology is Turning Electric Vehicles into Mortgage-Paying Assets
v2x.summary: V2G technology projections show potential for $200-1,000 monthly grid service payments, with ISO 15118-20 standard enabling mass-market charger production by 2028.

v2x.headline: Steve's 2026 V2G Predictions: The Year Bidirectional Charging Reshapes the Grid
v2x.summary: 2026 identified as V2G inflection point with first standards-based V2G-AC offering emerging, electric school buses uniquely positioned for grid services.

v2x.headline: EVs with Vehicle-to-Home Charging: 2026 V2H Guide
v2x.summary: V2H guide covers compatible EVs (Ford, GM, Tesla, Kia), required hardware systems, and safety protocols for homeowners wanting EV backup power.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid technology: How bidirectional charging transforms EVs into grid assets
v2x.summary: V2G enables mobile batteries to discharge during peak demand, with smart grid technology and advanced software facilitating grid energy flow optimization.

v2x.headline: Which Cars are V2G Capable? 2026 List & Guide
v2x.summary: 2026 V2G capabilities expand with BMW iX3, Mercedes-Benz GLC, Volvo EX90 launching, while Kia EV9 V2H and Renault partnerships enable grid integration.

v2x.headline: Blockchain and bio-inspired deep learning for energy-efficient EV-to-grid optimization
v2x.summary: Scientific research presents bio-inspired optimization framework using blockchain smart contracts to manage V2G charging/discharging schedules for EV fleets.

v2x.headline: What Cars Support V2G? A Guide to Bidirectional Charging EVs
v2x.summary: Guide clarifies V2G-capable vehicles including Ford F-150 Lightning, Kia EV9, Nissan Leaf, GM vehicles, Tesla Cybertruck with equipment requirements.

v2x.headline: What is V2H? How Vehicle-to-Home Charging Works
v2x.summary: V2H technology enables EVs to supply power to homes during outages via bidirectional chargers, with ISO 15118-20 standard vehicles coming 2026.

v2x.headline: Brownley Introduces Legislation to Promote Bidirectional Electric Vehicle Charging in the U.S.
v2x.summary: Congresswoman Brownley reintroduced Bidirectional EV Charging Act requiring all new EVs post-2029 support bidirectional charging for grid resilience.
